acceptance_criteria: Austerity changes at least one target outcome consistently across multiple seeds and calibrations.
The ABM exposes a policy channel that is interpretable in economic terms, not just a numerical switch.
JG experiments distinguish at least one meaningful wage or policy calibration effect beyond simple activation.
Plots and tests reflect the redesigned policy surface and no longer imply unsupported results.
constraints: This is exploratory/model-development work and is not required for publishing the current draft.
Do not reinsert ABM figures into the blog until the redesigned model is validated.
description: Redesign the ABM austerity mechanism so fiscal tightening actually binds in measurable ways across seeds, and extend the JG surface so comparisons can say more than activation versus no activation.
goal: Turn the ABM into a useful exploratory surface for fiscal tightening and Job Guarantee dynamics rather than an on/off toy.
implementation_notes: Superseded by task-019, which redefines the ABM work around explicit competing policy controllers rather than generic ABM redesign.
title: ABM fiscal-tightening and JG sensitivity redesign
